article_title: FDA Launches Real-Time Clinical Trials Pilot With AstraZeneca and Amgen, Aiming to End Years-Long Reporting Lags

content_preview.title: FDA Launches Real-Time Clinical Trials Pilot With AstraZeneca and Amgen, Aiming to End Years-Long Reporting Lags
content_preview.summary: The FDA announced two proof-of-concept real-time cancer trials and opened public comment on a broader pilot program, betting that cloud and AI-mediated data flow can compress drug review timelines.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The US Food and Drug Administration on April 28, 2026 unveiled what it described as the first concrete steps toward a long-promised overhaul of how clinical trial data reaches regulators. According to the [FDA's press release distributed via GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/28/3283099/0/en/FDA-Announces-Major-Steps-to-Implement-Real-Time-Clinical-Trials.html), the agency announced the successful initiation of two proof-of-concept "real-time clinical ...

editor_notes.content_quality: FDA RTCT pilot announcement, primary-sourced via fda.gov + GlobeNewswire FDA distribution + BioPharma Dive. All quoted material verbatim.

source_verification.https://www.fda.gov/news-events/press-an: source-0.html — primary FDA press release. All claims verified: AstraZeneca TRAVERSE Phase 2 mantle cell lymphoma trial with MD Anderson + UPenn, Amgen STREAM-SCLC Phase 1b small cell lung carcinoma trial with site selection in process, Makary 'For 60 years, we've been conducting clinical trials in the same way' quote verbatim, 'in real time as a trial progresses' verbatim, 'real-time, continuous trials across all phases of drug development' verbatim, May 29 RFI deadline, July selection criteria publication, August pilot selections.
source_verification.https://www.globenewswire.com/news-relea: source-2.html — same FDA press release distributed via GlobeNewswire. Identical content to source-0; the article uses this URL for inline links because it's allowlisted while fda.gov direct may not be.
source_verification.https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/fda-r: source-1.html — corroborates the AstraZeneca + Amgen partnership and the FDA's stated objective of compressing review-decision lag time.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified verbatim against the FDA primary source. The article omits the Amgen trial's name (STREAM-SCLC, mentioned in FDA release) but doesn't misattribute anything.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly catalogs the open questions (selection criteria, eligibility breadth, statistical-integrity questions, target review-time reduction).
editor_notes.originality: FDA RTCT initiative not previously covered. Cross-reference to RAPID coverage pathway (CMS-FDA, recently published) and National Priority Voucher program is appropriate context.
